---
title: "Internalizing Environmental Risk: Insurance Design and Firm Behavior in Hazardous Industries"
subtitle: ""
author: "Kaleb K. Javier"
format:
  beamer:
    template: "my-template.tex"
    keep-tex: true
    aspectratio: 169
    pdf-engine: xelatex
    header-includes:
      - |
        \providecommand{\estc}[1]{\textcolor[HTML]{1B7837}{#1}}
theme-bg-color: "0D2D52"
---



#

\LightMode
\centering

![](../../Data/tank_jpg.jpg){width="100%"}


---



#
\LightMode
\centering

![](../../Data/LUST.jpg){width="100%"}

---






---

## Does insurance design change firm behavior?

\BerkeleyLightMode

**Setting**

- USTs --- retail petroleum storage, $\sim$\,140,000 facilities, the most common source of groundwater contamination in the US (EPA 2024)
- Compulsory insurance the law since 1988 (RCRA Subtitle I, \$1M floor)
- **Clean shock:** Texas exits its flat-fee fund December 22, 1998 --- 26,000 facilities enter risk-rated private insurance overnight
- 9.8M tank-years of EPA panel data spanning both regimes

\vspace{0.2cm}
**Three questions**

- **Q1 --- Observability:** Is release risk predictable from observable tank characteristics?
- **Q2 --- Behavior:** Does risk-based pricing change closure, replacement, and release outcomes?
- **Q3 --- Welfare:** How much of the first-best welfare gain does risk-rated pricing capture?

---

## What is an underground storage tank?

\BerkeleyLightMode

[ADD FIGURE HERE]

---


## Related literature

\BerkeleyLightMode

\small

**Liability regimes, financial responsibility, and UST policy**

Shavell (1986, 2004, 2007); Yin, Kunreuther \& White (2011); Yin, Pfaff \& Kunreuther (2011, 2007); Boomhower (2019); Ho, Hsu, Cha \& Rivera (2018)

*First firm-level panel analysis of insurance design --- risk-rated vs. flat-fee --- as a financial responsibility instrument; clean policy shock; structural welfare decomposition*

\medskip

**Environmental economics and environmental risk**

Fowlie, Reguant \& Ryan (2016); Muehlenbachs (2015); Blundell, Gowrisankaran \& Langer (2020); Kellogg \& Reguant (2021)

*Dynamic structural model of firm behavior under environmental regulation; welfare decomposition bounding gap to first-best*

\medskip

**IO of selection markets: risk-based and cost-based pricing**

Einav, Finkelstein \& Mahoney (2021); Einav, Finkelstein \& Cullen (2010); Bundorf, Levin \& Mahoney (2012); Handel, Hendel \& Whinston (2015); Mahoney \& Weyl (2017)

*[PLACEHOLDER]*
